mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.21% as of Dec 2007 indicates easing cycle underway
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit stress
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in Dec 2007, near cyclical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or mechanism fails to predict recession-adjacent periods with Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, indicating weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 680591M in Dec 2007 reflects holiday season strength masking underlying weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS growth decelerates, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50,000 jobs per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling hidden labor market weakness that precedes broader economic contraction and negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50,000 while UNRATE remains below 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151980K in Dec 2007 shows slowing hiring momentum
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to outperform single-series PAYEMS signal with Rank IC difference < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,200 thousand units annually while retail sales remain flat, indicating synchronized demand collapse across residential and consumer sectors that drives negative equity returns as recession probability rises sharply over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1200 and RSAFS month-over-month growth is below 0.05%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1549K in Nov 2007 shows housing stress
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistical advantage over univariate HOUST signal (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.36 in Dec 2007 shows early signs of production slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI remains above 2.5% while federal funds rate falls below 2.0%, creating negative real rates that signal monetary desperation and financial system stress, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as investors flee risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.33 in Dec 2007 reflects elevated inflation
regime_hint: stagflationary stress with monetary policy constraint
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (negative real rates) shows no advantage over univariate FEDFUNDS signal with Rank IC difference < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth trajectory from prior quarter combined with current-month retail sales weakness signals demand momentum loss that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as consensus growth forecasts are revised downward and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate below 2.0% annualized and RSAFS month-over-month change below 0.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19727.92B in Q3 2007 shows slowing growth
regime_hint: demand-side growth deceleration in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no predictive advantage over univariate GDPC1 signal with Rank IC difference < 0.015.
